title: Eskaron

'''Eskaron''' was a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Genarius]].<ref name="LFC" />

Originally a habitable moon, Eskaron was perforated by long networks of tunnels created by [[Space worm/Legends|space worms]], which fed on a type of metal within the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. The [[Worm/Legends|worms]] exposed Eskaron's molten core which expanded and destroyed the local [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]], killing several of the worms. Once all of that metal was consumed, the space worms moved on, leaving behind only the tunnels and some [[The Worm|remains]].<ref name="LFC" />

The tunnels varied in size, from only a few [[Meter/Legends|meters]] up to several [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] wide. Most tunnels were large enough for [[Starship/Legends|starships]] to fly through, so organized races were set up by [[Riboga]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] since [[72 BBY/Legends|72 BBY]], an activity which continued after [[Nirama]] took over Riboga's organization in [[53 BBY/Legends|53 BBY]]. Nirama took a cut of the revenues, and also made bets on the results of the race. The races were active in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. The governor of [[Forard]] city, [[Klis Joo]], was building a racer for the Eskaron races as a hobby at that time.<ref name="LFC" />

The tunnels were also used to test piloting skills and the [[Blood Velkers]] gang held rites of initiation there.<ref name="LFC" />

In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], there was a track that youth gang [[Blood Raptors]] used while Nirama's races were being performed in another part of Eskaron. The Raptor track first went through [[The Spiral]] and, after a sharp turn, through the area known as [[Skinning the Mynock]], formed as two figure eights. It then crossed the track of Nirama's race and continued through the [[Cavern of Teeth]] and twelve zig-zag turns, before leaving the moon. The first racers could then choose between going to the [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]] and back, or visiting the city of [[Nub Saar]] before going back to the start point; any other racers were supposed to follow the chosen path.<ref name="POB">{{LivingForce|The Price of Business}}</ref>

At that point, during a race at that track between Raptor [[Gael Kinner]]'s [[G-59 Cannibalizer]] and [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] intimidated entrepreneur [[Teeloo]]'s [[YT-1210 light freighter/Legends|YT-1210]] ''[[Neeva-Beelo]]'', one of Nirama's racers was destroyed, killing pilot [[Arraxx]]. Immediately, Nirama asked for revenge against the responsible people, who were tracked after they finished the race.<ref name="POB" />
